topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on the screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. My breath hitched, a sudden, shallow catch in my chest. I hadn't expected it. A profile picture loaded: a familiar, sneering face, the one that had been synonymous with every awkward moment of my early years. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, the plastic cool beneath my touch. Every muscle in my jaw tightened, a hard knot forming just beneath my skin. *No. Absolutely not.* I quickly minimized the message, focusing instead on the dust motes dancing in the sliver of moonlight cutting across my bedroom floor. The silence in the room suddenly felt heavy, charged with unspoken history.

[story 2]

When the chime sounded, I froze mid-sip of my tea. The porcelain cup felt suddenly hot in my hand, and my gaze snapped to the phone screen. It was him. Mark. The sight of his name, paired with the familiar, mocking icon, sent a distinct shiver down my spine that had nothing to do with the room temperature. I gripped the phone so tightly my knuckles turned white. My heart began to beat a frantic rhythm against my ribs, a rapid drum solo I couldn't silence. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my small living room three times before collapsing back onto the sofa, staring blankly at the wall, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

I stared at the incoming request, my eyes tracing the familiar contours of his profile picture. A wave of immediate, unpleasant constriction washed over me, making my stomach clench uncomfortably. I consciously forced myself to breathe in, slowly, deliberately, trying to regulate the quickening pulse throbbing at my neck. My hands, resting on the desk, began to tremble slightly. What was he expecting? The anticipation of his message felt like a physical weight pressing down on my shoulders, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I picked up the device, my thumb hovering over the notification, my whole body coiled, waiting for the next inevitable move.
